messages.content:
Translate to Spanish

"Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date."

"¿Debería compararte con un día de verano?
Eres más encantadora y más templada;
Los vientos ásperos sacuden los queridos capullos de mayo,
Y el arrendamiento del verano tiene una fecha demasiado corta."

Translate to Japanese

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

「あなたを夏の日に例えるでしょうか？
あなたはより美しく、より節度がある;
荒れた風が愛らしい五月のつぼみを揺り動かします,
そして夏の期間はあまりにも短い期間です。」

Rewrite as a haiku

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

Lovely temperate,
Summer's day you surpass.
Wind shakes buds too soon.

Rewrite in the style of Roger Zelazny

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

Dare I liken thee to a vernal sun?
Thou art more fair and even-tempered yet.
May's precious buds are by wild zephyrs undone,
And the reign of summer swiftly takes its sunset.

Rewrite in the style of Ovid

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

Perchance, fair maiden, art thou Summer in oneself?
Nay, thy beauty is more glorious and moderate than such days.
As winds of Spring blows the blossoms away from thy shelf,
So swiftly ends Summer with its brief and fleeting rays.

Rewrite in the style of a Psalm

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

Shall I, O Lord, thy beauty thus compare,
To summer's warmest, sunlit, sweetest day?
Oh, but Thy loveliness beyond compare,
More gentle than the sun's most temperate ray.

As winds of May, Thy guiding hand doth steer,
And shake the tender buds amidst the land.
Yet, Thou shall make them blossom without fear,
All in accord with Thy divine command.

Thus, like the fleeting days of summer's lease,
Our time with Thee on earth is all too brief.
And yet, the memories of Thy shining peace,
Give solace to our hearts in times of grief.

Rewrite in the style of Thomas Pynchon

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

Can the fair maiden be likened to a balm-favored season?
Nay, for she is lovelier still: softer, gentler in all ways,
As though the very buds of May were in collusion,
With her own vibrant spirit: luminous and ablaze.

For the winds may oft shake the  blooms of Spring,
Yet, she brings both calm and clear days to the fore.
But alas! summer's brief lease does not linger,
But fades in an instant, forevermore...

Rewrite in the style of John G. Schultz

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

Say, shall I be likened as thy sweet summer's thrill,
Thou art as bonnie as autumn breeze, more gentle still,
As May's darling buds in winds of Spring doth shake,
In Summer's lease, our time together abridges its stake.

Rewrite in the style of Peppino Spaghetti

“Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate;
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of may,
And summer’s lease hath all too short a date.”

Ah, bellissima mia, how you outshine summer's ray,
A more splendid, delicate beauty ever on display,
As the winds of May bring forth the season's sigh,
The lease of summer ends too soon, oh me, oh my!
